rationale: The citing paper uses Reinartz, Krafft, and Hoyer (2004) to position a specific gap in CRM research, stating that prior CRM implementation work examined organizational alignment but did not address sales force incentive design. This citation is tied to the paper's core framing of why a salesperson-driven CRM setting with multidimensional incentives is needed. Even though the reference is brief, it plays a clear role in motivating the paper's contribution rather than serving as a generic background mention.
relation_description: The citing paper builds on the CRM implementation literature represented by Reinartz et al. (2004) and extends it by introducing incentive and private-information problems in salesperson-managed customer relationships. It uses the cited work as a prior boundary point and adds a new organizational mechanism domain (sales compensation) not covered there.
